title: How Deploying a Digital Store on GitHub Pages Works and How to Set It Up Today — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: You're a digital creator in Ethiopia facing a harsh reality: PayPal, Stripe, and other payment processors reject your account due to 'geographic restrictions.' You can't sell your ebooks, courses, or art without a payment processor, but you can't get approved. The frustration is real. You've tried everything: applying to multiple payment processors, using workarounds, and even begging for help. The problem persists. You need a solution that works. That solution is deploying a digital store on GitHub Pages, and we're here to guide you through it. No PayPal required. No hassle. No excuses.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

Deploying a digital store on GitHub Pages is a game-changer for creators like you who can't use traditional payment processors. It's a decentralized solution that puts you in control. You can sell your digital products without relying on a third-party payment processor. This method works because it bypasses the usual payment processor restrictions. You can focus on creating and selling your products without worrying about payment processing issues. It's a straightforward, no-hassle solution that gets you back to business.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you start, you'll need a GitHub account, a GitHub Pages site, and a payment processor like NOWPayments. You'll also need to set up your digital store using a platform like Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle. This bundle includes everything you need to get started, including a backend engine, frontend wallet integration, and an admin dashboard. Make sure you have all the necessary tools and knowledge before you begin. This will save you time and frustration in the long run.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

Setting up your payment and delivery pipeline is a crucial step in deploying your digital store on GitHub Pages. You'll need to integrate your payment processor, NOWPayments, with your digital store. This will allow you to accept payments from customers and deliver your digital products automatically. Make sure you configure your payment settings correctly to avoid any issues. You can do this by following the instructions in the Full Stack Bundle documentation.
Don't worry about payment processing issues – our solution bypasses them.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, you're ready to go live. Make sure you test your digital store thoroughly to ensure everything is working correctly. Once you're satisfied, you can launch your store and start selling your digital products. This is the moment you've been waiting for – making your first sale. Be prepared for a rush of excitement and a sense of accomplishment. You've worked hard to get to this point, and it's time to reap the rewards.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

Scaling your digital store is the next step after making your first sale. You'll need to monitor your sales and traffic to ensure your store can handle the demand. Make sure you have a plan in place to handle any issues that may arise. You can do this by setting up analytics and monitoring tools. This will help you identify any bottlenecks and make adjustments as needed. By scaling your store, you can increase your revenue and reach more customers.
How do I build a digital store that accepts crypto payments?

The most common question we get is: 'Will this method work for my specific situation?' The answer is yes. Our solution is designed to work with NOWPayments, which supports over 300 coins and has no KYC requirements. This means you can sell your digital products without worrying about payment processing issues. Our solution is flexible and can adapt to your unique situation.
You're in control with our decentralized solution – no third-party payment processors required.

Your Action Checklist

To deploy your digital store on GitHub Pages and start selling your digital products, follow these steps:

1. Create a GitHub account and set up a GitHub Pages site.
2. Choose a payment processor like NOWPayments.
3. Set up your digital store using Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle.
4. Integrate your payment processor with your digital store.
5. Configure your payment settings correctly.
6. Test your digital store thoroughly.
7. Launch your store and start selling your digital products.
8. Monitor your sales and traffic to ensure your store can handle the demand.
9. Set up analytics and monitoring tools to identify any bottlenecks.
10. Make adjustments as needed to scale your store and increase revenue.
